touches.ai_summary: Initial outreach via LinkedIn to Cameron Nkrumah introducing our call recording and coaching platform. Cameron responded positively, expressing interest in exploring solutions for their CS team's coaching workflows.

questions_asked.question: What makes your platform different from what's already out there?
questions_asked.answer: Our platform focuses specifically on post-call coaching workflows with automated highlight reels and manager review queues, designed for lean CS teams without dedicated QA resources.

next_steps: Schedule brief intro call to understand current coaching process

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email recapping LinkedIn conversation and proposing times for initial call. Cameron confirmed availability and mentioned they're currently evaluating a few options including Gong.

questions_asked.question: How long does the typical evaluation process take?
questions_asked.answer: Most teams complete technical evaluation in 2-3 weeks, but we're flexible based on your timeline and can accelerate if needed.

next_steps: Discovery call scheduled for next week

touches.ai_summary: Pre-call email with agenda and discovery questions. Cameron replied confirming the time and noted that Taylor Becker from IT would need to be involved for security review later in the process.
next_steps: Discovery call confirmed
Plan to loop in IT Director for technical review

touches.ai_summary: 34-minute discovery call with Cameron covering current coaching process, team structure, and pain points. CosmicConnect has 6 CS reps handling healthcare IT clients with complex technical support needs. Currently using manual call review which consumes 4-5 hours per week of manager time. Cameron is the primary champion but noted that final approval will require IT sign-off and budget approval from CFO.

questions_asked.question: Walk me through your current call review process - who's involved and how much time does it take?
questions_asked.answer: I personally review 2-3 calls per rep weekly, taking notes manually. It's eating up about 5 hours of my week and we're only covering maybe 15% of our call volume.

questions_asked.question: What outcomes are you hoping to achieve with a call recording solution?
questions_asked.answer: Faster rep ramp time, consistent coaching across the team, and honestly just getting those hours back in my week. We're growing and I can't scale the manual approach.

questions_asked.question: How does your pricing work for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: We have flexible packaging based on seat count and feature tier. For 6 seats with our automation features, we typically see deals in the $5-8K ACV range depending on configuration.

questions_asked.question: What does implementation look like and how long does it take?
questions_asked.answer: Standard implementation is 1-2 weeks including integrations and team onboarding. We provide dedicated support throughout and most teams are fully productive within 30 days.

objections: We're also looking at Gong which seems more established in the market
next_steps: Schedule technical evaluation demo
Send over case study from similar healthcare IT company
Prepare comparison with Gong for next conversation

touches.ai_summary: 47-minute technical evaluation and product demo with Cameron. Walked through call recording, AI-generated coaching summaries, and manager review workflows. Cameron was impressed with the automation capabilities and ease of use compared to Gong's interface. Discussed integration with their current phone system (RingCentral) and CRM (HubSpot). Cameron mentioned budget is tight this quarter and asked about pricing flexibility.

questions_asked.question: Can you show me how the automated highlight reels work for calls over an hour long?
questions_asked.answer: Demonstrated the AI summary feature that pulls key moments including objection handling, technical questions, and next steps - typically condensing 60min calls into 3-5 minute review clips.

questions_asked.question: How does the RingCentral integration work and is there any lag in call recording?
questions_asked.answer: Integration is native via API, recordings start automatically within 2-3 seconds of call initiation with no quality degradation. Showed live example of recent implementation.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: We'd like to have something in place by end of quarter, so probably need to finalize in the next 3-4 weeks. Budget cycle resets May 1st which could be helpful.

questions_asked.question: What kind of pricing flexibility do you have? Our budget is pretty constrained right now.
questions_asked.answer: We have some flexibility especially for right-fit customers. Let me understand your budget constraints better and we can work on a proposal that makes sense for both sides.

objections: Gong has more brand recognition and our CFO might be more comfortable with a known vendor
next_steps: Schedule meeting with executive decision-maker
Prepare custom pricing proposal
Send security documentation to Taylor Becker for IT review

touches.ai_summary: 54-minute executive meeting with Cameron to discuss business case and ROI. Cameron presented the use case but no executive sponsor joined the call. Reviewed time savings analysis showing 4-5 hours/week recovered manager time and estimated 20% faster rep ramp. Cameron expressed concerns about getting CFO approval without exec sponsorship and mentioned Gong's pricing came in lower than expected. Discussed need to move quickly due to end of quarter timing.

questions_asked.question: Help me understand the decision-making process - who needs to sign off on this purchase?
questions_asked.answer: Ultimately it's our CFO, but I have budget authority up to $5K. Above that I need to build a business case. IT Director Taylor also needs to approve any new software vendors.

questions_asked.question: What would make this a no-brainer decision for your CFO?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, a compelling ROI story and a price point that doesn't require executive escalation. If we can stay under my approval threshold that would help a lot.

questions_asked.question: Can you get us closer to the $4K range? That would make this much easier to push through.
questions_asked.answer: Let me take this back to my team. That's a significant discount from our standard pricing but I understand the budget constraints. I want to make this work if we can.

questions_asked.question: What's Gong's pricing looking like for comparison?
questions_asked.answer: They came in around $7K for our seat count, but Cameron mentioned they're willing to negotiate. However, our platform's automation features are more aligned with their lean team structure.

next_steps: Prepare revised pricing proposal targeting sub-$5K threshold
Schedule pricing review call
Follow up with Taylor Becker on security review status

touches.ai_summary: 22-minute pricing call with Cameron to review custom proposal. Presented revised pricing at $3,900 ACV (36.9% discount) for 6 seats with unlimited automation tier. Cameron appreciated the flexibility and confirmed this fits within approval threshold. Discussed payment terms (annual upfront vs quarterly). Cameron mentioned Taylor completed security review with no blockers. Still no executive sponsor engaged but Cameron confident in ability to close without escalation.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: For this pricing level we typically require annual commitment paid upfront, but we can discuss a quarterly payment schedule with a slight adjustment to the total annual value.

questions_asked.question: Is there anything else you need from us to move forward with this pricing?
questions_asked.answer: I need to run the final numbers by our CFO informally just to make sure there are no surprises, but at this price point it should be straightforward. Taylor's already approved from the IT side.

next_steps: Cameron to discuss with CFO informally
Schedule final negotiation call to close on payment terms
Prepare contract for signature

touches.ai_summary: 36-minute negotiation call to finalize terms and payment structure. Cameron pushed for quarterly payment option citing cash flow concerns. Agreed to $3,900 ACV with quarterly billing at $1,025 per quarter ($4,100 total annual value). Cameron confirmed CFO informal approval and Taylor's IT sign-off complete. Discussed implementation timeline and onboarding process. Cameron mentioned Gong is still in consideration but our pricing and automation features are compelling advantages.

questions_asked.question: What's the earliest we can start implementation if we sign this week?
questions_asked.answer: We can kick off implementation within 3-5 business days of contract signature, with full onboarding completed in 2 weeks. Your team could be live by early May.

questions_asked.question: What would it take to get this signed by end of week?
questions_asked.answer: I need to get the quarterly payment terms documented and run the final contract by our CFO for signature authority confirmation. Assuming no issues, I can commit to signature by Friday.

questions_asked.question: Can you include 2 additional admin seats at no charge for our managers?
questions_asked.answer: The 6 seats include full admin access for managers, so you're already covered there. All seats have the same permission levels and can be assigned to reps or managers as needed.

objections: Still comparing with Gong's final offer which came in at $6,200 with some additional features
next_steps: Send updated contract with quarterly payment terms
Cameron to obtain CFO signature
Schedule implementation kickoff call

touches.ai_summary: 10-minute executive check-in call with Cameron. Cameron confirmed CFO reviewed contract and requested one minor change to payment schedule (net-30 terms instead of due on receipt). Discussed competitive position vs Gong and Cameron confirmed our automation features and pricing make us the preferred option. Contract ready for signature pending payment terms adjustment.

questions_asked.question: Can we add net-30 payment terms to each quarterly invoice?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can accommodate net-30 terms. I'll have the updated contract to you today with that adjustment.

next_steps: Send final contract with net-30 payment terms
Cameron to route for CFO signature
Target close by end of week
